---
title: "Research"
---

## Working Papers

:::: {.research-item}

### Rational Inattention in Wage Growth Expectations

::: {.research-meta}

**Draft:** Coming soon
<!-- **Draft:** [PDF](paz-rational-inattention-wage-expectations.pdf){target="_blank"} -->
<!-- [Paper](paper.pdf){target="_blank"} · [Slides](slides.pdf){target="_blank"} · [Replication files](https://github.com/USERNAME/REPOSITORY){target="_blank"} -->

:::

Using data from the New York Fed's Survey of Consumer Expectations, I document that wage growth expectations remain anchored and unresponsive even during inflationary episodes in which inflation expectations respond strongly. To explain these patterns, I develop a rational inattention model embedded in a standard New Keynesian framework with price and wage rigidities. Households cannot disentangle two structural shocks that have similar effects on inflation but opposite effects on wage growth. As a result, their inferred effects on expected wage growth partially offset, generating muted wage growth expectations consistent with the survey evidence.

::::

## Work in Progress

:::: {.research-item}

### Memory, Frequency Bias, and Inflation Expectations

::: {.research-meta}

<!-- **Draft:** In progress -->

:::

<!-- Briefly describe the research question, methodology, and expected contribution of the project. Two or three sentences are generally enough for a work in progress. -->

::::

:::: {.research-item}

### Subjective Inflation-Unemployment Beliefs

::: {.research-meta}

**With:** [Anushka Mitra](https://anushkamitra.com/){target="_blank"} and [Prachi Srivastava](https://srivastava-prachi.github.io/){target="_blank"}
<!-- **Draft:** In progress -->

:::

<!-- Briefly describe the project here. -->

::::

:::: {.research-item}

### The Macroeconomic Impacts of Weather Shocks: Insights from the ENSO Phenomenon

::: {.research-meta}

**Draft:** Available upon request.

::: 

::::
